definition: The 5y5y forward at ~4.5% (±50bp) functions as a nonlinear financial conditions tightening threshold transmitted through corporate refinancing (BBB coupon jump from ~3.0-3.5% to 5.5-6.0%), mortgage transmission (30Y fixed at 6.2-6.5%), and pension/insurance duration demand withdrawal.
evidence: Four agents independently identify the ~4.5% level
Historical analogues: 6.5% mortgage rate in 2006, 3.0% 10Y in 2018
SVB precedent: HTM losses at 10-15% of par
Pension/insurance reflexive loop: higher discount rates reduce duration demand which raises rates further
r-star decomposition (r* + 2.5% breakeven + term premium) has substantial component uncertainty
open_questions: Is a smooth increase in elasticity around 4.0-5.0% more defensible than a discrete threshold?
If r-star is 1.5% vs 2.0%, the threshold shifts by 50bp — how should this uncertainty be handled?
Does Woodford (2003) support discontinuity or merely state-dependence?
